rationale: The cited study is about competitive advertising interference on packaged-goods sales, while the citing paper’s meta-analysis explicitly targets firm-value outcomes and includes only studies with firm-value dependent variables, so this citation is unlikely part of the core analyzed sample. But the evidence is sparse (only a single DOI match with no in-text context), and it could still serve as mechanism/background support for advertising-effect heterogeneity, so it is not safe to drop outright.
